center.title: Welcome to FileUni Settings Center
center.subtitle: Finish the first-time settings to get started

guide.title: Finish first-time settings in three simple steps
guide.desc: If this is your first time, keeping the default choices is usually the best option. FileUni starts with a beginner-friendly setup, and you can fine-tune everything later.
guide.card1Desc: Pick the single runtime directory where FileUni should keep its settings and data.
guide.runtimeDirChangeHint: To change this location, restart FileUni with --runtime-dir <DIR>. Service installation uses the same flag.
guide.card1Hint: Restart FileUni after changing the runtime directory.
guide.card2Desc: Set a password for the administrator account you will use later.
guide.card3Desc: When the initial settings are done, return to the launcher and start FileUni.
guide.recommendedDesc: If you are unsure, keep the current defaults. They are the easiest choice for personal use, home use, and quick trials.
guide.locationDesc: This is only where FileUni will save things for you. You do not need to create any files by hand.
guide.requiredPrompt: FileUni has not finished the initial settings yet. Open Settings Center now?

editor.title: Settings Center
editor.rawHiddenTitle: Edit the config text only if you need more control
editor.rawHiddenDesc: During the initial settings, the quick settings flow above is usually enough. Open advanced settings only if you need more detailed control.
editor.finishHint: Confirm to finish the initial settings.
editor.moreActionsDesc: License, storage, and external tool checks can usually wait until after setup is finished.

locked.title: Settings Center Unavailable
locked.howToUnlockArg: Settings Center opens automatically when `install.lock` is missing.

profile.title: Select Performance Profile
profile.subtitle: Choose a starting point based on your hardware. You can customize everything in the next step.

low.name: Eco / IoT
low.desc: Perfect for Raspberry Pi or 1GB VPS. Lightweight SQLite & In-memory cache.

medium.name: Standard
medium.desc: Balanced setting for Home NAS. Reliable and efficient metadata handling.

high.name: Performance
high.desc: Production grade. Best with PostgreSQL & Redis for massive file clusters.

config.title: Environment Settings
config.subtitle: Configure your database and cache. DSN strings will be built automatically.
config.dsnNote: Note: Manual edits to the DSN will override form values.
config.kvDashmapHint: Note: In-memory cache is volatile and cleared on restart.
config.kvSqlHint: Note: Will reuse primary database for cache storage.

externalHints.redis: Use Redis when you already operate a Redis deployment.
externalHints.valkey: Valkey is the recommended first-install choice for an external cache server.
externalHints.keydb: KeyDB fits high-concurrency and multithreaded workloads better.

storagePool.title: Storage Pool Settings
storagePool.desc: Choose a backend type for each pool, then fill only the fields that backend really needs.

s3Hints.endpoint: Enter the S3 or compatible object storage endpoint.
s3Hints.region: Enter the service region or provider-specific region value.
s3Hints.bucket: Enter the bucket used as the root for this storage pool.
s3Hints.access_key_id: The access key identifier used to sign requests.
s3Hints.secret_access_key: The secret key used to sign requests.

dropboxHints.access_token: Optional short-lived token. Prefer refresh_token for long-term use.
dropboxHints.refresh_token: Recommended for long-term Dropbox access. OpenDAL refreshes access tokens automatically.
dropboxHints.client_id: Dropbox app client ID used with refresh_token.
dropboxHints.client_secret: Dropbox app client secret used with refresh_token.

onedriveHints.access_token: Optional short-lived Microsoft Graph token. Prefer refresh_token for long-term use.
onedriveHints.refresh_token: Recommended for long-term OneDrive access. Include offline_access when obtaining it.
onedriveHints.client_id: Microsoft Graph app client ID used with refresh_token.
onedriveHints.client_secret: Optional for public clients; required for confidential clients.

gdriveHints.access_token: Optional short-lived token. Prefer refresh_token for long-term use.
gdriveHints.refresh_token: Recommended for long-term Google Drive access. OpenDAL refreshes access tokens automatically.
gdriveHints.client_id: Google OAuth client ID used with refresh_token.
gdriveHints.client_secret: Google OAuth client secret used with refresh_token.

webdavHints.endpoint: Enter the base WebDAV service endpoint.
webdavHints.username: The account used to sign in to WebDAV.
webdavHints.password: The password used to sign in to WebDAV.

storageCache.title: Read / Write Cache Acceleration
storageCache.writeRisk: Warning: when write cache is enabled, data is cached first and flushed back asynchronously. Sudden power loss, cache cleanup, or flush failure may cause file loss or delayed persistence.

admin.title: Set up the administrator account
admin.subtitle: This is the account you will use to sign in to the admin console.
admin.pendingHint: Applies when the initial settings are finished
admin.resetRuleHint: If no administrator exists yet, apply will create an admin user named admin. If an administrator already exists, apply will reset the first administrator password to the password entered here.
admin.resetSuccessWithUser: Admin password applied. Current admin username: {{username}}
admin.finalConfirmDesc: Confirm to save your settings and finish the initial settings. You can adjust more options later.
admin.passwordHintTitle: Set the administrator sign-in password before finishing
admin.passwordHintDesc: You will use this password the first time you sign in to the admin console.

final.title: Settings complete
final.subtitle: Administrator account {{user}} is ready.
final.adminCreatedDefault: Created administrator {{user}} with the temporary password {{password}}. Please change it after your first sign-in.
final.adminReset: Updated the sign-in password for administrator {{user}} to {{password}}.
final.adminExisting: Administrator {{user}} already exists, so the password was left unchanged.
final.step1: Return to the launcher and start FileUni.
final.step2: Open the Web UI and sign in with the administrator account.
final.step3: Use the admin console later if you want to adjust more settings.
final.openHint: Start FileUni, then open the Web UI to sign in.
final.runningHint: FileUni is already running, so you can open the Web UI right away.

logs.title: Real-time Deployment Log
logs.applyingConfig: Applying configuration and syncing DB schema...

settings.title: System Settings
settings.noData: No data available for this section
settings.navNote: Modifying system parameters will take effect immediately for the runtime config, but some core engines may require a restart.
settings.licenseNote: License keys are bound to the specific hardware fingerprint of this server. Changing significant hardware components may require a new license key.
settings.generalNote: Changes to core engine parameters may require a system restart to take full effect. Always validate your TOML structure before making direct edits.

config.title: System Configuration
config.resetNotice: Template loaded. Review and save to apply changes.
config.resetHint: Reset restores the default settings. Save when you are ready.
config.restartNotice: Some settings require a server restart to take full effect.

thumbnail.title: Thumbnail Settings
thumbnail.subtitle: Configure the built-in image pipeline, desktop-only external tools, and video frame capture strategy without editing TOML by hand.
thumbnail.imageBackendHint: Built-In uses the Rust thumbnail pipeline and is the default. External uses libvips or ImageMagick and is only needed when you explicitly want the external toolchain.
thumbnail.imageSmallSkipMb: Return Original Image Below This Size (MB)
thumbnail.imageMaxSizeMb: Skip Image Thumbnails Above This Size (MB)
thumbnail.videoSmallSkipMb: Skip Video Thumbnails Below This Size (MB)
thumbnail.videoMaxSizeMb: Skip Video Thumbnails Above This Size (MB)
thumbnail.videoSeekHint: If ratio is set, it overrides seek seconds. A default like 0.3 usually avoids black opening frames.
thumbnail.videoHardwareHint: If "Enable Hardware Encoding" is turned on in Video Transcoding, desktop video thumbnails automatically reuse the same hardware backend and device path to try hardware-assisted frame extraction first. If that path fails, FileUni falls back to the software path automatically. No second hardware config is needed here.
thumbnail.videoHardwareReuseActive: Current status: video thumbnails will try the {{backend}} hardware backend first with device {{device}} and fall back to software frame extraction automatically on failure.
thumbnail.videoHardwareReuseInactive: Current status: video thumbnails are not reusing a hardware backend and will use software frame extraction directly.
thumbnail.pdfOfficeHint: PDF, document, and LaTeX thumbnails reuse the raster toolchain. Documents also need LibreOffice, and LaTeX also needs latexmk.
thumbnail.pdfUsesRasterToolsHint: PDF thumbnails reuse the shared libvips / ImageMagick raster toolchain above.
thumbnail.officeUsesLibreofficeHint: Document thumbnails convert the source through LibreOffice first, then continue through the PDF thumbnail path.
thumbnail.latexUsesPdfHint: LaTeX preview thumbnails use latexmk to generate a PDF first and then reuse the PDF thumbnail parameters for the final thumbnail.
thumbnail.model3dUsesBlenderHint: 3D model thumbnails use Blender in the background to import the model and render a still view. Current support: obj, stl, gltf, glb.
thumbnail.pdfSmallSkipMb: Skip PDF Thumbnails Below This Size (MB)
thumbnail.pdfMaxSizeMb: Skip PDF Thumbnails Above This Size (MB)
thumbnail.officeSmallSkipMb: Skip Document Thumbnails Below This Size (MB)
thumbnail.officeMaxSizeMb: Skip Document Thumbnails Above This Size (MB)
thumbnail.latexDependsOnPdfHint: LaTeX preview thumbnails generate a PDF first and then reuse the PDF thumbnail parameters and toolchain above. Even if PDF thumbnails are disabled, that PDF parameter group still affects LaTeX preview thumbnails.
thumbnail.helper: Desktop systems use FFmpeg for video thumbnails. Android and iOS use the system media framework instead. External tool paths are still used for PDF, Office, text, and optional external image rendering.

mediaTranscoding.title: Video Hardware Transcoding
mediaTranscoding.subtitle: Configure WebUI HLS transcoding, the shared FFmpeg path, and hardware backend parameters.
mediaTranscoding.applied: Video transcoding settings written into the editor
mediaTranscoding.applyFailed: Failed to apply video transcoding settings
mediaTranscoding.enableHint: This switch decides whether WebUI video playback goes through the transcoding pipeline. When disabled, playback keeps using the original file directly. When enabled, FileUni generates HLS output.
mediaTranscoding.allowSoftwareFallback: Fall Back to Software When Hardware Fails
mediaTranscoding.videoHint: Phase one handles video only. These fields control the output format and quality. In most cases, the defaults are the right choice.
mediaTranscoding.hardwareHint: This switch only controls whether FileUni tries GPU hardware encoding first. When disabled, software CPU encoding still works. For Linux Intel/AMD, start with vaapi and /dev/dri/renderD128.
mediaTranscoding.hardwareAllowFallback: Fall Back to Software When Hardware Fails
mediaTranscoding.platformLinux: Linux Intel/AMD: prefer vaapi, with /dev/dri/renderD128 in most cases.
mediaTranscoding.platformWindowsIntel: Windows Intel: try qsv first; the device field is usually left empty.
mediaTranscoding.platformWindowsNvidia: Windows NVIDIA: try nvenc first; the device field is usually left empty.
mediaTranscoding.platformWindowsAmd: Windows AMD: try amf first; the device field is usually left empty.
mediaTranscoding.platformMacos: macOS: prefer videotoolbox and leave the device field empty.
mediaTranscoding.platformFreebsd: FreeBSD: if VAAPI is confirmed to work on the host, try vaapi + /dev/dri/renderD128; otherwise keep hardware encoding disabled first.
mediaTranscoding.probeOk: Test passed: {{backend}} can be used for {{encoder}} video encoding
mediaTranscoding.probeFailedEncoder: Test failed: FFmpeg does not provide encoder {{encoder}} required by {{backend}}
mediaTranscoding.probeFailedBackend: Test failed: encoder {{encoder}} was found, but {{backend}} could not complete a real encoding run
mediaTranscoding.probeConstraintHint: This hardware requires encoding dimensions within width {{minWidth}}-{{maxWidth}} and height {{minHeight}}-{{maxHeight}}. Check drivers, device permissions, and the FFmpeg hardware path.
mediaTranscoding.helper: The backend test runs the configured FFmpeg binary with the selected encoder and a minimal validation workload. Test FFmpeg first, then test the backend.

compression.title: Compression & Extraction
compression.subtitle: Configure the 7-Zip dependency used for compression, extraction, archive browsing, and related enhancements.
compression.applied: Online compression settings written into the editor
compression.enableHint: When enabled, FileUni can create archives, extract more formats, and browse supported archives. Without 7-Zip, support falls back to native ZIP/TAR only.
compression.helper: Check 7-Zip first, then save your settings.
compression.disabledHint: Online compression is currently disabled in this draft. Users will not see archive actions until you enable it and save the config.

globalModeHint.disabled: Turning this off makes all existing protected data temporarily unavailable.
globalModeHint.obfuscate: Only obfuscated storage stays available; existing encrypted data becomes temporarily unreadable.
globalModeHint.encrypt: Only encrypted storage stays available; existing obfuscated data becomes temporarily unreadable.

modeSummary.disabled: The system keeps the configuration only. Users cannot create or access protected directories until a mode is enabled again.
modeSummary.obfuscate: The system now uses high-performance obfuscation. It hides names, extensions, and obvious content signatures, but it is not strong encryption.
modeSummary.encrypt: The system now uses server-side transparent encryption. Directory keys are wrapped on the server, and all protocols still read logical plaintext through VFS.

risks.disabled: Warning: switching to Disabled does not migrate or restore protected data. Existing protected data stays unavailable until a matching mode is enabled again.
risks.obfuscate: Warning: switching to Obfuscate does not convert existing encrypted data. Data written under another mode becomes temporarily unavailable.
risks.encrypt: Warning: switching to Encrypt does not convert existing obfuscated data. Data written under another mode becomes temporarily unavailable.

obfuscation.title: Obfuscation Settings
obfuscation.blockPresetHint: Start with a common preset. Only switch to a custom value when your hardware or range access pattern really needs it.
obfuscation.blockSizeHint: The practical range is usually 128-1024 KiB. Larger values improve sequential throughput but make small range reads coarser.
obfuscation.blockSizeInvalid: Enter an integer block size greater than 0.
obfuscation.blockSizeRecommended: The current value {{value}} KiB is within the recommended range.
obfuscation.blockSizeCustom: The current value {{value}} KiB is valid, but outside the common recommended range.
obfuscation.prngHint: xorshift is lighter, pcg is steadier. Both apply to obfuscation mode only.
obfuscation.workersPresetHint: 0 means auto-select based on CPU cores. Use other values only when you want to cap parallelism manually.
obfuscation.workersHint: Use 0 to auto-select based on CPU cores. This only affects large obfuscation reads.
obfuscation.workersInvalid: Enter an integer worker count greater than or equal to 0.
obfuscation.workersAutoDetail: Auto mode is enabled. Parallelism will be selected from available CPU cores.
obfuscation.workersRecommended: The current value {{value}} is within the recommended worker range.
obfuscation.workersCustom: The current value {{value}} is valid, but you should confirm device load before using it.

encrypt.title: Encryption Settings
encrypt.cipherHint: Phase 1 is fixed to AES-256-CTR with server-side encryption and decryption.
encrypt.wrapKeyHint: Used to wrap each user's random protected-directory encryption key. Minimum 32 characters. Changing it makes existing encrypted data temporarily unreadable.
encrypt.wrapKeyValid: Current length {{count}}. Ready for production use.
encrypt.wrapKeyInvalid: Current length {{count}}. At least 32 characters are required.

externalTools.autofillSuccess: Detected paths have been filled into the form.
externalTools.autofillNoChange: No detected path was available to autofill.
externalTools.mobileDisabled: Android and iOS keep image thumbnails on the built-in Rust pipeline and use system media frameworks for video thumbnails. External desktop binaries such as FFmpeg, ImageMagick, LibreOffice, libvips, and 7-Zip remain unavailable, so leave tool-backed features disabled on mobile deployments.

messages.writeFailed: Failed to write external dependency configuration
messages.ffmpegTestReview: Test completed: FFmpeg is available, but follow-up review is recommended
messages.toolTestReview: Test completed: {{tool}} is available, but follow-up review is recommended

config.noteEmpty: No note available for the current key.
config.noteHint: Notes map to config keys and sections.

free.title: Free
free.desc: Core features for personal or light usage.

premium.title: Premium
premium.desc: Unlock advanced features and higher limits.

storage.title: Storage Pools & Backends
storage.subtitle: Visual editor for vfs_storage_hub storage configuration

main.hint: Quick edit for the primary storage
main.desc: This view only edits the primary connector of the default pool. Use Advanced to manage pools/connectors/policies.
main.noPrimaryConnector: The default pool has no primary_connector configured. Select one first, or fix it in Advanced mode.

policies.title: Storage Policies
policies.hint: Optional: map role_id to pool_name, quota, and private remote mount limits. If empty, default_pool is used and self-service mounts stay disabled.

root.fs: Local directory path. On mobile, it must be under the RUNTIMEDIR sandbox.
root.memory: Memory backend root is only used to satisfy config validation.
root.ios_scoped_fs: iOS security-scoped bookmark; must start with bookmark_b64:.

errors.parseRoot: TOML root must be an object
errors.defaultPoolNotFound: default_pool '{{name}}' must match an existing pool name

quickSettings.title: Quick Settings
quickSettings.subtitle: Walk through the common choices used during the initial settings.
quickSettings.parseErrorTitle: The current settings cannot be shown in simplified mode right now.
quickSettings.parseErrorHint: You can still edit them below. Nothing will be lost.
quickSettings.setupHintTitle: Recommended: keep the defaults if you're unsure
quickSettings.setupHintDesc: The default settings already work well for most first-time installs, and you can change everything later.

fields.licenseInputPlaceholder: Paste your Base64 license key here...
fields.licenseHint: Get your license key from the official website. The device code is used for hardware binding.

hints.sqliteSingleNode: Recommended for first-time setup, personal use, and home devices.
hints.sqlitePathOnly: Only the database file path is needed here. The connection string is handled automatically.
hints.cacheDashmapLightweight: Recommended for first-time setup and light use. No extra service is required.
hints.cacheNoExternalConnection: This option does not need a separate cache service. Leaving it as-is is usually fine.

setupAdvanced.title: Need a more advanced connection setup?
setupAdvanced.desc: Open these advanced options only if you want to connect FileUni to an existing database or cache service.

setupHints.externalDatabase: If you already have a separate data service, just enter its address, port, and sign-in details here.
setupHints.externalCache: If you already have a separate cache service, just enter its address, port, and sign-in details here.

advancedActions.intro: You can adjust more settings later in the admin console.
advancedActions.licensePlaceholderHint: Open license management to review your current status and key.

performance.intro: If you are unsure, keeping the default choice is fine. These options simply tell FileUni what kind of device and usage style fits you best.

preview.simpleHint: Here are the settings that will be applied.

descriptions.constrained: For tighter devices such as routers, mini hosts, or older ARM hardware, prioritizing lower background load and memory usage while preserving core usability.
descriptions.lightweight: For reasonably capable devices such as laptops or older PCs, balancing feature completeness and resource usage as much as possible.
descriptions.performance: For high-memory, high-compute devices such as desktops, workstations, or newer servers, prioritizing richer features and higher throughput for heavier workloads.

scenarioHint.line1: Choosing a usage scenario lets FileUni apply a generally good tuning profile for most environments. In most cases, you do not need to adjust settings manually.
scenarioHint.line2: If you are familiar with the config options, you can switch to text editing mode for finer tuning.

loadProfile.title: Performance Device Plan
loadProfile.desc: Choose whether this performance-class machine mainly serves a small number of users or needs to absorb more concurrent access.
loadProfile.lowConcurrencyDesc: Keep rich features enabled, tuned for personal use, small teams, or admin-heavy operation.
loadProfile.highConcurrencyDesc: Increase connection limits, queue sizes, and background throughput for busier simultaneous traffic.
loadProfile.multiUserDesc: Increase connection limits, queue sizes, and background throughput for busier simultaneous traffic.

preheatMode.title: Captcha Preheat Strategy
preheatMode.desc: Balances captcha pool memory usage and burst-time generation throughput. This strategy automatically tunes pool and concurrency values.

descs.memory: Reduce preheat pool and concurrency for lower memory usage
descs.balanced: Balance memory usage and response speed
descs.throughput: Increase pool and concurrency to absorb traffic bursts

performanceTips.title: Performance Tuning Advice
performanceTips.raid: Consider RAID arrays for improved disk I/O performance and data redundancy
performanceTips.pgsql: Tune PostgreSQL parameters (shared_buffers, work_mem, effective_cache_size) to match hardware resources
performanceTips.memory: This module provides basic optimization suggestions; complex scenarios require system-level tuning

flowStartup.title: Follow Startup
flowStartup.preStartupHint: Execute in order before FileUni starts
flowStartup.preStartupDelayHint: After the pre-startup commands are launched in order, wait this many seconds before continuing FileUni startup. Use 0 for no extra wait.
flowStartup.postStartupHint: Execute in order after FileUni starts
flowStartup.postStartupDelayHint: After FileUni is ready, wait this many seconds before launching post-startup commands in order.
flowStartup.securityTitle: Security Warning: Do not run FileUni as root for follow-startup commands
flowStartup.securityBody: These commands run with the same OS user as the FileUni process. If FileUni runs as root, both saved commands and test actions become root-level command execution. Always run FileUni as an unprivileged user.
flowStartup.testBehaviorHint: The test button only checks whether a command can be launched within a short window. Long-running processes are stopped automatically after the verification window so the test does not leave background processes behind.
flowStartup.testResultStdoutTruncated: Stdout was truncated and is not shown in full.
flowStartup.testResultStderrTruncated: Stderr was truncated and is not shown in full.
flowStartup.statusRunning: Launched successfully and kept running during the test window
flowStartup.noCommands: No commands configured, click Add Command to start
flowStartup.argsHint: Each line becomes one argument. Spaces inside the same line are preserved.

common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this item?
common.name: Name
